## @jgengine/core/multiplayer/chatContract

- `ChatActions` (interface): interface ChatActions ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ChatSendArgs` (interface): interface ChatSendArgs ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ChatSendOutcome` (interface): interface ChatSendOutcome ‚Äî ‚öÝ undocumented
- `ChatSync` (interface): interface ChatSync ‚Äî Callback seam for backends that cannot host React hooks (e.g. the ws client): subscribe delivers the channel's recent history on every change; send resolves with the host's verdict.
- `ChatTransport` (interface): interface ChatTransport ‚Äî Backend seam for remote text chat, mirroring PresenceTransport: the use* members are called as React hooks by consumers, so a mounted transport must never change identity ‚Äî remount the subtree to switch backends. useMessages returns undefined while the subscription is loading and the channel's recent history once live.

- `PresenceTransport` (interface): interface PresenceTransport<TRow, TLocation, TGameId extends string = string> ‚Äî Backend seam for multiplayer presence. Feeds are reactive data and change identity whenever any player's pose updates; actions MUST be identity-stable for the lifetime of a mounted session so join/leave lifecycle effects can depend on them without re-running per pose tick. The use* members are called as React hooks by consumers, so a mounted transport must never change identity ‚Äî remount the subtree to switch backends.

## @jgengine/node/devSavePlugin

- `EDITOR_SCENE_FILENAME` (const): const EDITOR_SCENE_FILENAME: "editor.scene.json" ‚Äî Filename editor scene documents are saved under inside a game's `src/`.
- `SAVE_ENDPOINT_PATH` (const): const SAVE_ENDPOINT_PATH: "/__jgengine/save" ‚Äî Route the dev save middleware answers on; matches `installSaveEndpoint` defaults.
- `devSavePlugin` (function): function devSavePlugin(resolveSrcDir: SrcDirResolver): DevSavePluginShape ‚Äî Dev-only Vite middleware that gives the F2 chord family its write path: editor mode's Save (Ctrl+S / `save_scene`) writes `editor.scene.json` and debug mode's Tune "Save to source" rewrites tunable literals, both into the directory `resolveSrcDir(gameId)` returns.
- `handleSaveRequest` (function): function handleSaveRequest(resolveSrcDir: SrcDirResolver, body: string): SaveEndpointResponse ‚Äî Applies one parsed save request against a resolved game `src/` directory.
- `standaloneSavePlugin` (function): function standaloneSavePlugin(rootDir: string = process.cwd()): DevSavePluginShape ‚Äî `devSavePlugin` preset for a standalone game project (one game, `src/` at the project root): every gameId maps to `<rootDir>/src`. Drop into the scaffolded `vite.config.ts` plugins array.

## @jgengine/node/editorHostPlugin

- `EditorManifestAsset` (interface): interface EditorManifestAsset ‚Äî One placeable model the standalone editor lists ‚Äî a stable id and a URL the dev server serves it from.

## @jgengine/ws/worldHost

- `WorldGameHost` (interface): interface WorldGameHost extends GameHost ‚Äî A {@link GameHost} whose worlds run on `HostedWorldSession`s; `tick` advances them and re-broadcasts on change.
- `WorldGameHostOptions` (interface): interface WorldGameHostOptions ‚Äî Config for {@link createWorldGameHost}: how to resolve a hosted world's authoritative session per server.
- `createWorldGameHost` (function): function createWorldGameHost(options: WorldGameHostOptions): WorldGameHost ‚Äî The GameContext-loop counterpart of the reducer `createGameHost`: a structural {@link GameHost} that serves each world's full `WorldSnapshot` as `serverState`, so the existing ws router, `createWsBackend`, and the shell's `attachWorldSync` carry host-authoritative GameContext worlds with zero changes to any of them. The harness owns the tick cadence (call {@link WorldGameHost.tick} on an interval); commands and joins broadcast immediately.
